display_name: Barman / pgBackRest (PostgreSQL physical backup catalog + pg-backup-api)
ai_scope_discriminator: Barman/pgBackRest are generic Postgres physical-backup catalogs; they hold AI/ML data ONLY when the backed-up cluster runs pgvector (or an ML schema). The /diagnose JSON does NOT dump table contents, so scope must be inferred from metadata, not records. In-scope gate (read-only, all from /diagnose): (1) per-server config block names a database/cluster whose name signals embeddings/RAG/vectors (e.g. server names or conninfo dbnames like 'rag', 'embeddings', 'vectorstore', 'llm', 'pgvector', 'langchain', 'llamaindex', 'chroma_pg', 'supabase'); (2) the server's 'postgres' status block exposes pg version + loaded extensions where 'vector' / 'pgvector' / 'pg_embedding' / 'lantern' appears in available/installed extensions -> physical backups of that cluster carry the vector columns; (3) backup labels / WAL paths in the catalog reference vector-pipeline app names. If none of these markers appear it is plain DB substrate (PII/PHI maybe, but NOT AI/ML scope per feedback_ai_llm_scope_not_substrate) and falls OUT of Cat-13 AI scope. The discriminator is the pgvector extension presence in the diagnose 'postgres' block, not the existence of a backup.
fingerprint_markers: GET /status returns the exact 3-byte body "OK" (literal double-quoted string, content-type application/json) - unique to pg-backup-api

GET /diagnose returns barman-diagnose JSON whose top-level keys include 'global' and per-server objects containing 'config', 'backups', 'wals', and a 'status' sub-block with 'pg_version'/'current_xlog'
404 responses are JSON shaped {"error": "404 Not Found: ..."} (Flask errorhandler in pg-backup-api), not an HTML 404
Server header is the Werkzeug/Flask dev server (app.run) or gunicorn; combined with /status "OK" + 7480 it is pg-backup-api, not a generic Flask app
fp_guards: port:7480 alone is low-entropy; any other Flask/JSON app can bind 7480. EXCLUDE by requiring GET /status to return the literal body "OK" AND GET /diagnose to return barman-unique key 'barman_ver' (or 'pgespresso_installed'). Generic Flask apps will 404 on /diagnose.

Catch-all/honeypot pages (LBot fleet, Insight #107/#108) can echo "OK" on any path. GUARD: confirm /diagnose returns barman_ver AND /status returns ONLY the 3-byte "OK" with nothing appended; a catch-all that returns "OK" everywhere also returns "OK" on a random nonexistent path -> probe a junk path and require a JSON 404 {"error":...}, not another "OK".

pgBackRest itself has NO HTTP listener (CLI + TLS server on 8432 for repo-host SSH-replacement, protocol is pgBackRest binary, not REST). Do NOT dork pgBackRest as an HTTP REST surface. A 7480 hit is pg-backup-api (Barman side) only; pgBackRest exposure is S3/Azure/GCS bucket listings, not a port.

Generic PostgreSQL on 5432 / pgvector-enabled Postgres is NOT this platform - that is the substrate being backed up, not the backup catalog. Scope only the 7480 pg-backup-api host or the object-store repo, never the live DB.

Barman 'pgespresso_installed' is a stable diagnose key even on modern versions (value false); it is barman-namespace and will not appear in unrelated services, so it is the safest single discriminator.

cves.desc: pg_dump/pg_dumpall/pg_restore (used by Barman/pgBackRest logical-backup pipelines) embed untrusted superuser-controlled data as psql meta-commands; restoring a poisoned backup executes arbitrary code as the restoring client OS account (CVSS 8.8) - a restore-time RCE on any exposed backup that can be fed to a restore.

Improper newline handling in pg_dump object names enables client-side code execution and SQL injection on the target during restore of a crafted backup - chains with an exposed/writable backup catalog.

pivot_paths: GET /diagnose -> single read enumerates ENTIRE catalog: every managed server name, conninfo, backup_directory, full backup list with backup_id/size/begin_time/end_time/WAL ranges, retention policy, and the 'status' block (pg_version + extensions) - this IS the list-backups move, no per-server call needed
From /diagnose JSON, read each server's installed/available extensions to apply the pgvector AI-scope discriminator (names-only, no record read)
GET /servers/<server_name>/operations -> list prior recovery/config operation IDs (reveals operator restore activity + server names) - read-only enumeration
GET /servers/<server_name>/operations/<operation_id> -> read status of a known operation (DONE/FAILED/IN_PROGRESS) - metadata only

For barman-cloud / pgBackRest S3 repos (no port): if the bucket is public, an unauth GET on the repo prefix (s3://bucket/...?list-type=2 or the public https index) lists backup.info / backup_label / *.manifest object KEYS - names only, never GET the object body
restraint_note: Names-only. The whole finding is the /diagnose metadata: server names, backup IDs, sizes, timestamps, retention, pgvector presence. STOP there. Forbidden: (1) downloading any backup object, WAL segment, base.tar, or S3 *.manifest body = exfiltration of the protected cluster; (2) issuing POST /servers/<name>/operations or POST /operations (recovery/config_switch/config_update) = a write/exec primitive that spawns a subprocess server-side and, per CVE-2025-8714/8715, can be a restore-time RCE chain - this is a write, never to be touched on the survey set. Enumerate the catalog, count backups, note pgvector scope, log to nuclide.db. Blocked = 'surface open, access not exercised'.
